steps.title: Use angle sum to find the third angle in each triangle
steps.content:
In any triangle, the sum of angles equals \$180°$.

For triangle $DEF$: $$F = 180° - 47° - 97° = 36°$$ For triangle $RST$: $$T = 180° - 47° - 97° = 36°$$

steps.title: Identify corresponding angles from the given information
steps.content: We are explicitly told that angle $D = 47°$ and angle $R = 47°$, and that angle $E = 97°$ and angle $S = 97°$. This directly establishes the angle correspondence: $D \leftrightarrow R$ and $E \leftrightarrow S$.

steps.title: Apply AA similarity criterion
steps.content: Two triangles are similar if two pairs of corresponding angles are equal (AA similarity). We already know that $D = R = 47°$ and $E = S = 97°$. This is sufficient to conclude similarity—we do not need to verify that $F$ and $T$ are equal, since that follows automatically from the angle sum property. That matches choice D — D is correct.
